explanation: The command substitutes '/Change' with '/C' (equivalent short option) and adds an extra space between '/C' and '/TN' for insertion. This maintains the same functionality while altering the command structure.
This is the original command line. It is included to show that the rule can detect the standard form of the command.
Single quotes are used instead of double quotes to enclose the task name. This is a valid syntax variation on Windows command lines.
The leading backslash in the task path is omitted, but the command is still valid if the task name is known to exist in the default task folder.
Extra spaces are inserted between the command-line arguments. This is a safe mutation that does not affect the command's functionality.
